Description: A rugged ascent up the Quitasol massif, avoiding main trails.

Terrain & Technical Info: Exposed, steep, loose dirt, very hot at base.
Description: Classic steep climb dominating the north of the valley.
Wildlife refuge where the Medellín River is born.

Terrain & Technical Info: Páramo ecosystem, steep ridges, high wind.
Description: The only páramo in the immediate Aburrá Valley.

Terrain & Technical Info: Slippery rocks, dense vegetation, ropes needed.
Description: Classic local adventure trail requiring agility.

Terrain & Technical Info: Humid forest, muddy spots, river banks.
Description: Beautiful waterfall tucked inside the Ayurá river basin.

Terrain & Technical Info: Very steep, loose dry dirt, exposed to sun.
Description: The most popular quick fitness hike in the city.
Iconic viewpoint over the northwest of Medellín.

Terrain & Technical Info: Urban trail, stone paths, steep stairs.
Description: Trail crossing the Arví park buffer zone and Comuna 8.

Terrain & Technical Info: Well-maintained dirt roads and forest paths.
Description: Excellent for bird watching and easy weekend hikes.

Terrain & Technical Info: Muddy descents, humid, inside a cave.
Description: A waterfall that drops directly through a hole in a cave roof.
Massive waterfall deep in the mountains of Jardín.
The world's highest natural pyramid. Brutal gradient.

Terrain & Technical Info: Dense forest, very steep continuous incline.
Description: Prominent peak offering panoramic views of the Cauca River.

Terrain & Technical Info: Technical rock scrambling, dense cloud forest.
Description: Massive granite formations, highly demanding.

Terrain & Technical Info: Deep mud, extreme weather, steep rock.
Description: Highest point in Antioquia. Demanding frailejones trek.

Terrain & Technical Info: Steep descent, humid and slippery rocks.
Description: Towering waterfall in the indigenous territory of Támesis.
Easy walk connecting the viewpoint to a natural pool.
Trek through the Tapartó reserve looking for spectacled bears.
Ancient trade route passing near Cerro Tusa.

Terrain & Technical Info: Cloud forest to páramo, misty, humid.
Description: Quieter alternative to Belmira with endemic flora.
Deep canyon exploration, less crowded than Melcocho.
Famous for crystal clear emerald waters.
Secluded waterfalls hidden deep in tropical forest.

Terrain & Technical Info: Steep descent to waterfall base, cables.
Description: Massive 90m waterfall in a dramatic canyon.
Walk around the base of the famous monolith.

Terrain & Technical Info: Pine forest, dirt roads, steady incline.
Description: Panoramic views of the San Nicolás valley.
Series of cascading waterfalls in a humid region.
Beautiful river hike with multiple natural pools.
Scenic walk through traditional ceramic-producing farmland.
Great training route with valley views.
Good intermediate hike south of the city.
Pleasant hike leading to a hidden waterfall in Guarne.

Route Name: La Casa en el Aire (Access trail)
Description: Trail leading down into the spectacular Buey River canyon.
Path through the San Sebastián de la Castellana reserve.
Old heritage trail connecting to the valley.

Terrain & Technical Info: Steep pine forest, slippery when wet.
Description: Prominent peak offering sweeping views of the Oriente.
A refreshing hike starting from Barro Blanco.
Classic páramo route passing Laguna San José.
A smaller, less crowded version of the Peñol rock.
Extensive walks through the high-altitude dairy region.
Hike to the base of one of Antioquia's highest waterfalls.
Tough climb marking the transition to the North plateau.
Great spot for a swim after a humid hike.
A series of powerful waterfalls in the northern valley.

Terrain & Technical Info: Dry, very hot, ancient stone paths.
Description: Old Spanish road connecting the colonial capital.
Walk along the Cauca river to the historic suspension bridge.
A tough, rarely visited peak in the coffee region.
Tough ascent from the south valley, great for stamina.
Beautiful natural pool surrounded by tropical vegetation.
Walk through historic railway infrastructure.
Exploring the spectacular marble canyon of Rio Claro.

Terrain & Technical Info: Slippery cave floors, dark, river wading.
Description: Cave exploration requiring headlamps and water shoes.
Great training route above the city with panoramic views.
Descent on an old path leading to beautiful falls.
Lush reserve in the mountains above Jericó.
Classic hike offering views of the Cauca river canyon.
Easy walk from the heritage town of Concepción.
Challenging route offering views of three different hills.

Route Name: Laguna de Santa Rita & Cerro Caramanta
Terrain & Technical Info: Deep mud, dense cloud forest transitioning to páramo, high humidity and rain probability.
Description: A highly demanding multi-day expedition leading to a pristine high-altitude lagoon, offering tough acclimatization terrain.

Terrain & Technical Info: Paved & dirt paths, well-marked, some stairs.
Description: The largest urban park in Medellín with a 5km circuit around one of the city's iconic hills. Perfect for daily exercise and sunset views.

Terrain & Technical Info: River tracing, forest trails, slippery rocks.
Description: A hidden gem in San Antonio de Prado following a pristine creek through dense forest. Multiple small waterfalls and natural pools.

Terrain & Technical Info: Dirt roads, open fields, exposed sections.
Description: Training route popular with runners offering spectacular views over Medellín's eastern hills. Best visited early morning or late afternoon.

Terrain & Technical Info: Cloud forest, muddy sections, steep inclines.
Description: A mystical cloud forest hike in Santa Elena. Named after local legends of witches gathering at the summit. Rich in birdlife and orchids.

Terrain & Technical Info: Jungle paths, river wading, butterfly-rich areas.
Description: A stunning waterfall surrounded by hundreds of butterflies in the humid forests of San Carlos. The pool is perfect for swimming.

Terrain & Technical Info: Dirt roads, pine forest, gentle inclines.
Description: A popular training hill for local cyclists and runners. The summit offers panoramic views of the San Nicolás Valley and José María Córdova airport.

Terrain & Technical Info: Steep grass and dirt paths, exposed summit.
Description: A challenging but rewarding climb in La Ceja. The summit features a large cross and 360° views of the surrounding dairy farms and rolling hills.

Terrain & Technical Info: Tropical forest, multiple stream crossings, wildlife.
Description: A beautiful canyon hike following a cascading stream through primary forest. Excellent for birdwatching and photography.
A series of spectacular cascades and deep pools in San Rafael's tropical rainforest. One of Antioquia's best kept secrets.
A short pilgrimage to the iconic Christ statue overlooking Marinilla. Perfect for families and sunset visits.

Terrain & Technical Info: Páramo wetlands, muddy, cold, high winds.
Description: A remote glacial lake hidden in the páramo above Belmira. Requires good physical condition and warm clothing. The frailejones are spectacular.

Terrain & Technical Info: Steep forest to páramo transition, muddy.
Description: A demanding climb from cloud forest to páramo. The summit offers breathtaking views of the northern valleys on clear days.

Terrain & Technical Info: Open pastures, dirt roads, cold climate.
Description: A peaceful high-altitude walk through dairy farms and rolling hills. Excellent for acclimatization before more challenging peaks.

Terrain & Technical Info: Steep descent, rocky trail, lush vegetation.
Description: A powerful 70m waterfall accessible via a steep but beautiful descent. Local legend says the mist has healing properties.
True to its name ("Cloud Mountain"), this trail winds through ethereal cloud forest with constant mist. The view over the Cauca Canyon is unforgettable.

Terrain & Technical Info: Dirt roads, coffee plantations, rural landscapes.
Description: A hike through traditional coffee farms and small villages. Learn about coffee production while enjoying stunning valley views.

Terrain & Technical Info: Dense jungle, steep sections, river crossings.
Description: A true expedition to a remote waterfall that drops into a pristine pool. The trail requires machetes during rainy season. Only for experienced hikers.

Terrain & Technical Info: Muddy, humid, river crossings, slippery rocks.
Description: Despite its unappealing name ("The Dirty Waterfall"), this is one of Jardín's most beautiful cascades. The trail is muddy but rewarding.

Terrain & Technical Info: Dry forest, rocky, exposed, hot climate.
Description: A steep climb offering incredible views of the Cauca River and the colonial town below. Best done at sunrise to avoid extreme heat.

Terrain & Technical Info: Dry, dusty, panoramic, some steep sections.
Description: A scenic trail along the rim of the Cauca River canyon. Spectacular views of the winding river below and the western mountains.

Terrain & Technical Info: River tracing, flat, some rocky sections.
Description: A short hike leading to a beautiful natural pool perfect for swimming. Very popular on weekends. The water is crystal clear and warm.

Terrain & Technical Info: Ancient cobblestones, dry forest, historical markers.
Description: A well-preserved Spanish colonial road connecting two historic towns. Follow the same path muleteers used 300 years ago.

Terrain & Technical Info: Dark caves, headlamp required, slippery floors.
Description: A limestone cave system with impressive stalactites and stalagmites. Home to bats and swiftlets. Headlamp and helmet mandatory.

Terrain & Technical Info: Forest trails, elevated wooden walkways, easy.
Description: A family-friendly trail in the Río Claro Reserve featuring elevated walkways through pristine forest. Great for birdwatching and butterflies.

Terrain & Technical Info: Tropical forest, river tracing, some bouldering.
Description: A beautiful waterfall deep in the tropical forest. Local legend says a mermaid lives in the pool. Best visited with a local guide.

Terrain & Technical Info: Jungle paths, river crossings, slippery sections.
Description: Not to be confused with the Jardín waterfall. This remote cascade in San Roque is a hidden gem of the Nordeste region. Very few tourists.

Terrain & Technical Info: Forest, steep inclines, muddy, some scrambling.
Description: A challenging climb to one of the highest peaks in the Nordeste region. The summit offers views all the way to the Magdalena River valley.
An extended version of the classic tunnel hike. This route passes through two historic railway tunnels along the abandoned Medellín-Puerto Berrío line.
A pristine waterfall in the remote municipality of Yalí. The name means "The Sunset" because the falls face west and catch golden afternoon light.
